C-CODEX-PRESET-LIMITS.IntentSummary: Replace ambiguous vv-openai and vv-gpt naming with canonical vv-codex and vv-codex-gpt names under provider.openai, apply explicit Codex subscription token budgets, preserve the old patch-provider spelling as a compatibility route, and synchronize public documentation, defaults, completions, tests, and governed artifacts.

Baseline.Note: Managed aliases currently use vv-gpt-* names under provider.openai and omit limit.input, allowing direct-API models.dev values to be inherited.
Resolved OpenCode metadata currently gives GPT-5.6 aliases input 922000, so proactive compaction waits near 902000 although Codex subscription input is limited to 372000.
The current canonical OpenAI preset is vv-openai, while vv-osovv and vv-osovv-cheap also reference vv-gpt-* aliases.
patch-provider openai currently targets provider.openai and conservatively merges managed aliases without deleting sibling models or options.

Target.Note: Canonical managed preset and alias names explicitly communicate Codex subscription semantics while retaining provider.openai interoperability.
Every managed Codex alias specifies context, input, and output so compaction behavior cannot depend on inherited API metadata.
Fresh configurations and documentation use vv-codex; legacy user-owned vv-openai or vv-gpt-* entries are not destructively removed.
patch-provider codex is canonical, with openai retained only as a compatibility spelling resolving to the same patch.

T-001.Title: Rename and correct managed Codex model aliases in patch-provider.

AcceptanceCriteria.Criterion: OPENAI_PATCH exposes only canonical vv-codex-gpt-* aliases for fresh patch application.
GPT-5.4 mini and GPT-5.5 aliases explicitly use 400000 context, 272000 input, and 128000 output.
GPT-5.6 Terra and Sol aliases explicitly use 500000 context, 372000 input, and 128000 output.
Reasoning effort, reasoning summary, encrypted reasoning content, underlying API model IDs, and conservative merge behavior remain intact.
codex is the canonical patch-provider name and openai remains a compatibility alias resolving to the same provider.openai object patch.
MODULE_CONTRACT, MODULE_MAP, and CHANGE_SUMMARY remain truthful.

T-002.Title: Rename the canonical preset and all managed role references to vv-codex terminology.

AcceptanceCriteria.Criterion: The built-in registry contains vv-codex instead of canonical vv-openai.
vv-codex, vv-osovv, and vv-osovv-cheap reference openai/vv-codex-gpt-* aliases where applicable.
Default vvoc configuration and bootstrap paths seed canonical vv-codex names and model references.
Preset ordering and completion output remain deterministic.
No unrelated provider presets or role assignments change.

Verification.Command: bun test src/commands/preset.test.ts src/commands/completion.test.ts src/commands/config-validate.test.ts src/commands/launch.test.ts

T-003.Title: Synchronize documentation and regression coverage for canonical Codex naming and compatibility behavior.

AcceptanceCriteria.Criterion: README examples use vv-codex, patch-provider codex, and openai/vv-codex-gpt-* identifiers.
Tests assert complete model objects including explicit input limits and canonical names.
Tests prove patch-provider openai compatibility without making it the canonical completion/list name.
Tests continue proving conservative first-apply and reapply behavior with unrelated provider fields preserved.

T-004.Title: Keep GRACE graph and verification projections truthful for the changed public behavior.

AcceptanceCriteria.Criterion: Inspect owning graph and verification entries and update them only if module purpose, paths, dependencies, or verification contracts changed materially.
No semantic anchors are renamed or removed.
The active change spec and plan remain valid GRACE 4 artifacts.
Targeted patch-provider, preset, completion, config validation, and launch tests pass.
bun run check and bun run build pass with fresh output.
The final diff contains only approved source, test, documentation, and GRACE artifact changes.
Any external GRACE status tooling failure unrelated to repository content is reported explicitly.

Verification.Command: bun test src/commands/patch-provider.test.ts src/commands/preset.test.ts src/commands/completion.test.ts src/commands/config-validate.test.ts src/commands/launch.test.ts
